asset_type: photorealistic residential background plate with current official product renders
prompt: Create a clean, photorealistic editorial background for a TaraHome technical blog cover about choosing between Matter-over-Thread and Matter-over-Wi-Fi smart plugs in Home Assistant. Use case: product-led smart-home compatibility article cover. Composition: 3:2 landscape, 1536x1024 if supported. Warm, lived-in residential utility corner or calm living-room console scene, photographed straight-on at a gentle eye-level angle. Include a broad pale-oak or medium-oak console surface and a softly textured warm off-white wall, with two believable grounded wall-outlet areas or enough uncluttered foreground space to later composite two real smart-plug product cutouts, one on the left and one on the right. Keep the center and the central 16:9 crop useful for an article card. Natural late-morning window light, realistic soft shadows, restrained dark-green accent from a small plant or ceramic object, subtle cream and wood palette, high-end but attainable homeowner setting, practical rather than staged luxury. This is a BACKGROUND PLATE ONLY. Do not create, depict, hint at, or invent any smart plugs, electronics, hubs, routers, phones, tablets, screens, cables, labels, logos, text, letters, numbers, icons, diagrams, network meshes, routing paths, arrows, dotted lines, chart elements, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, headline posters, or fake hardware. No people or hands. No strong depth blur. No vignette. No decorative tech graphics. Leave clear grounded placement zones for two product cutouts with natural contact shadows to be added later. Ensure objects do not overlap those placement zones.
composite_note: The generated background plate was combined with unmodified official IKEA GRILLPLATS and Shelly Plug US Gen4 product renders. Only background removal, isolation, proportional resizing, positioning, and natural contact shadows were applied to the source-backed hardware.
